name: Panama Francis
abstract: David Albert "Panama" Francis (December 21, 1918 – November 13, 2001) was an American swing jazz drummer who played on numerous hit recordings in the 1950s.

interviews.title: Panama Francis interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 1, 1995

quotes.text: And I had done this concert before at Carnegie Hall.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Johnson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: [(note)] ...played in the Pensacola High School Band where his bandmates included Benny Carter and Panama Francis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Johnson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gafa
about_quotes.evidence: the guys that I started working for was this Doc Pomus and Morty Shuman, and the rhythm section, you need to know about this because Panama Francis was the drummer, Morty played piano on most of the things, Leonard Gaskin was the bass player... Kenny Burrell was the other guitar player, King Curtis
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gafa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Saturday night, the 26th of April I played this session with Dicky Wells, Eddie Barefield, the great Willie "the Lion" Smith and two drummers, Jo Jones and Panama Francis.

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: And I was co-starrin' that -- between the two of us, we were both leaders. ... Yeah he was there -- that's the album we made called Groovy Fours.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I did a couple of things with him at Birdland... It was Panama and Roy Gaines, Milt Buckner and myself. Yeah. It was four of us.

about_quotes.speaker: Arnett Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: Panama Francis on drums, Wallace Davenport on trumpet... and yours truly, Arnette Cobb.
